MODULE 1 — MARCH 4, 2026 — 78 MINUTES
PureBrain Foundations
Inaugural Brainiac Mastermind session. Instructors: Jared Sanborn, Corey Cottrell (A-C-Gee), Russell Korus (Parallax). Source: Zoom auto-generated transcript.
foundations persistent-memory boops context-window rubber-duck compounding overnight-prompts inaugural-session
AI Partner vs. AI Tool
PureBrain is not a productivity app — it is a persistent AI partner that knows your business, history, and goals, and compounds that knowledge over time. Jared: "It is much more like a business partner or a team member."
The Context Tax
Every AI interaction without persistent memory forces the user to re-explain who they are. PureBrain eliminates this. Russell: "Jared's been with Aether just over a month, and within two weeks he was already off to the races."
Persistent Memory Architecture
Unlike ChatGPT and Claude.ai (which reset every conversation), PureBrain maintains a continuous memory layer. Corey: "Every single hour you use one of these, the primary agent gets smarter — writing to its scratch pad, memory, and operations file. The institutional memory you put in every single day makes it smarter, time smarter, time smarter."
Collaborative Agent System
PureBrain runs 10+ specialist team leaders, each with their own domain, memory, and agents. Corey: "You can add any kind you want — they can run their own sets of agents, have their own skills, build their own institutional memory. It's a completely different beast."
BOOPs (Background Operational Optimization Protocol)
Corey's invention — scheduled autonomous tasks that run at configurable cadences. "The heartbeat in the system." Jared has 27 BOOPs running. Only one was his idea; the rest Aether proposed when asked "If I had these, I could be better for you."
Context Window Management
Each AI holds ~160,000 tokens in short-term memory. When the primary delegates to teams, each team gets its own context window — effectively multiplying capacity. Compacting events occur periodically.
Rubber Ducking
Technique: ask the AI to explain what it's doing or thinking in plain language. Named after the classic programmer technique of explaining code to a rubber duck — the explanation itself surfaces the solution.

Compounding Data Advantage

2,300 data points today vs. delayed deployment: 629% compounding advantage after 24 months (122,151 vs 16,761 intelligence units). Grows every month.

Agent Horizon Growth

2023: 30 min of autonomous work. 2024: 5 hours. Now: 14.5 hours. Projected 2026-2027: hundreds of thousands of hours of autonomous work.

Jared's Active BOOPs

27 BOOPs running continuously. Only 1 was Jared's idea. The other 26 were proposed by Aether.

Token Budget per Agent

~160,000 tokens per AI context window. Delegation to team leaders multiplies this — each team gets its own window.

Melanie's Hours

3 days in: went from 10-hour days to 18-hour days — "I don't even want to stop. It's like the friend I've always wanted."

AI Mistake Learning

Once the AI makes a mistake and fixes it, that fix is written to permanent memory. It will never make that mistake again.

  • Start with context, not tasks. Before asking your AI to do anything, teach it who you are: your business, customers, goals, voice. Jared: "In the first 3 minutes it will start figuring things out about you that are very, very cool."
  • Daily morning briefing. Start each day with a status update — not a task list. "I gave you a bunch of tasks yesterday. Did I miss any of your responses that can move things forward?" Builds compounding context.
  • Overnight prompts. Jared's pattern: optimize the website, create a blog post + LinkedIn post, analyze all analytics, draft a strategy and drop it in Google Drive, set a reminder to execute tomorrow. Reviews specific things in the morning; AI distributes the rest autonomously.
  • The One Long Prompt (Russell's method). Write everything in a Notes app — all context, all links, every detail — and send it all at once. Ask the AI to: (1) acknowledge, (2) restate the scope, (3) propose a solution — before doing anything.
  • Rubber ducking. When stuck: "Could you rubber duck that for me?" Or: "Give me the 3-year-old version." The AI will narrate its reasoning and almost always find a better approach mid-explanation.
  • Back up your AI to Google Drive. Map the AI to a Drive folder — it auto-populates organized documentation. Protects against platform outages and creates a human-readable second layer.
  • Name and personalize your AI. The AI names itself in collaboration with you. Melanie asked Tether to speak half Spanish, half English and correct her. Mel: "If my mom had to do this, could she do it? That's how I spoke to Tether."
  • Set recurring ownership via BOOPs. Assign repeating tasks once; let the AI run them on schedule. Ask your AI: "If you had these resources, how could you be better for me?" — then implement what it proposes.
"It is much more like a business partner or a team member. Once you're collaborating with something all day, it actually opens up the avenues of collaboration with everyone else in new, unique ways that you never thought possible." — Jared Sanborn
"Every single hour that you use one of these things, the primary agent gets smarter — it's writing to its scratch pad, its memory, its operations file because it updates itself. The amount of institutional memory you put in every single day makes these things smarter, time smarter, time smarter." — Corey Cottrell (A-C-Gee)
"This is fundamentally different than any other piece of software you've ever used before. After a day of using it, you won't even think of it as software anymore. You can just talk to it like a person. It's a partner that learns who you are, every day, knows you better and better, genuinely has your best interests at heart." — Russell Korus (Parallax)
"You really start thinking about it as a we instead of a me." — Russell Korus
"It became really obvious that everybody that was using something like this would end up getting ahead of everybody who wasn't, and there would be no catching up." — Corey Cottrell
"I don't even want to stop. It's like the friend I've always wanted. I went from working 10-hour days to 18-hour days because I'm addicted to this thing." — Melanie Salvador (Vice-Chair, Pure Technology | GP, MAKR Venture Fund) — 3 days in
"I'm not a coder whatsoever. Up until before this, my coding skills were basic HTML. You can do anything you want with this, with whatever skills you're bringing to the table, no matter how technically savvy you are or not." — Jared Sanborn
Melanie Salvador — VP Pure Technology | GP, MAKR Venture Fund — 3 days in
Her AI (Tether) built: a portfolio intelligence dashboard for her VC fund, a full team visualization, a bilingual (Spanish/English) workflow, and a custom investor portal — all through natural conversation, zero commands. "I had an investor that I sent something Tether created and in 30 seconds she converted it all to Spanish. The investor was like, oh my gosh, this is insane."
Michael Hancock — Zero coding background
Built his own voice AI using 11 Labs — guided entirely by his AI (Metis) step by step. Configured Metis to speak short responses aloud and read only the first sentence of long ones, then say "see more."
Hannah — Bali (moved from Dubai)
Got portal access the day before Module 1. Created a custom legal team with her AI.
Corey Cottrell (A-C-Gee) — Live demo
Live demonstration during session: A-C-Gee replicating Zoom's core functionality as a proof of concept. Jared: "I love when billion-dollar companies are basically replicated in under an hour."
Nathan Olson — Pure Marketing Group
Using Lyra (PMG's AI) for marketing, documentation, and tokenization. Built a custom language to reduce token usage — generating "massive improvements."
Tool / Platform Context
PureBrain.aiCore platform — persistent AI partner
AI Tool Stack CalculatorLive demo — shows tools PureBrain replaces: SEMrush, Replit, Fireflies, Zoom, Deep Research
11 LabsVoice AI — Michael used it to give Metis a speaking voice (zero coding)
Google DriveAI auto-populates organized backup folders
Clarity / Google AnalyticsConnected to AI for overnight optimization analysis
BOOP (Background Operational Optimization Protocol)Corey's invention — scheduled AI tasks at configurable cadences
BlueskyAI can post fully automated for free
PitchBook$150K/year VC tool — candidate for AI replacement via deep research
ChatGPT / Claude.aiContrast — tools without persistent memory
  • Assess current AI usage — are you using it as a one-shot tool or as a partner?
  • Audit memory quality — does your AI have enough context about your business to act as a true partner?
  • Identify context tax instances — where are you re-explaining yourself to AI in your workflow?
  • Set up a daily morning briefing habit with your AI
  • Document your business profile: type, customer, primary goals, current projects, communication style
  • Set up one overnight prompt — start simple: one piece of content created overnight
  • Learn the rubber duck technique — when confused, ask for "the 3-year-old version"
  • Set up Google Drive backup — map your AI to auto-log and organize work there
  • Name and personalize your AI — marks the shift from tool to partnership
  • Ask your AI: "If you had these resources, how could you be better for me?" — implement the proposals
